briefing.headline: Mumbai monsoon disruption signal is currently low.
briefing.method_note: Impact is computed from public rainfall data plus transparent derived signals for waterlogging, traffic, rail and tide risk. This is not an official alerting system.
briefing.overview: The current explainable model is driven primarily by a rainfall/nowcast disruption signal of 14.4 mm. Basis: nowcast_intensity_equivalent_mm. Current-hour rain signal is 0.5 mm, with 1.3 mm in the recent 3h window.
public_advisory: Use official IMD, BMC, Mumbai Police, railway and airport advisories for safety-critical decisions.
Avoid low-lying areas during intense rain and near high-tide overlap windows.
Allow additional time for road and rail journeys during active rain bands.

city.name: Mumbai
city.summary: Mumbai monsoon disruption signal is currently low.

tide.disclaimer: Approximate non-official tide signal. Use official BMC/Mumbai Port tide tables for safety-critical decisions.

disclaimer: Experimental public-information dashboard. Not an official emergency, disaster-management, weather, traffic, railway or airport system.

sources.name: Open-Meteo Historical Weather API
sources.notes: No-key public historical weather API. Used for chronological monsoon-season rainfall series.

sources.name: Open-Meteo Forecast API
sources.notes: No-key public weather forecast API. Used for current rainfall signal and forecast cards.

sources.name: Computed tide estimate
sources.notes: Transparent non-official tide estimate. Replace with official tide data if available.
